title: Group Travel in Nishi-Shinjuku: A Private Base Beneath Tokyo's Skyscrapers
excerpt: Why Plat House Nishi-Shinjuku's walkable location, right beneath the skyscraper district, suits groups who want to be close to everything.
contentHtml:
Nishi-Shinjuku is Tokyo's skyline in miniature — a dense cluster of skyscrapers that includes the Tokyo Metropolitan Government Building, all within walking distance of Shinjuku Station. Staying here puts your group in one of the most convenient, urban parts of the city.

A Private House Within Walking Distance of Shinjuku Station

Plat House Nishi-Shinjuku is a full house rental within walking distance of Shinjuku Station, giving your group a shared kitchen, living space, and laundry facilities rather than a subdivided hotel floor.

Set Up for the Whole Trip

Netflix and a Nintendo Switch handle the downtime, and the kitchen and washing machine mean your group can cook and do laundry rather than relying entirely on restaurants for a multi-day stay in one of Tokyo's most expensive dining areas.

Skyscrapers by Day, Neon by Night

The free observation deck at the Tokyo Metropolitan Government Building offers city views without an entrance fee, and Shinjuku Central Park is a quick walk for some greenery between sightseeing stops. Shinjuku Takashimaya Times Square covers shopping, and Golden Gai's tiny bars in Kabukicho are an easy trip for an evening out.

Book Direct and Save

Booking directly with Plat House Nishi-Shinjuku beats typical OTA pricing, with free cancellation up to 7 days before check-in.

Check Availability & Book Now
